name: Extracellular vesicle and secretome therapies surveillance pass for June 15, 2026

scope.question: What changed for Extracellular Vesicle and Secretome Therapies since the June 6, 2026 no-op surveillance pass, and does any change justify a public update?

summary: Started as a stale registry cleanup for the four EV/secretome public human study records with missing registry_last_checked metadata. Direct registry/source checks found no result-bearing change for the Alzheimer's adipose MSC exosome trial, the hUC-MSC exosome knee osteoarthritis pilot, the placental MSC-EV knee osteoarthritis randomized trial, or the Plexoval II platelet-EV phase I wound study; the staged bundle refreshes their registry metadata. Same-window PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, and web checks found a June 2026 Frontiers in Aging accepted article for NCT07322224, a single-arm open-label healthy-aging pilot combining lifestyle optimization, supplements, and autologous pro-regenerative conditioned media. Because that paper adds a human biological-age biomarker signal outside the existing skin-aging-only wording, the pass staged a public outlook refresh. The staged outlook keeps evidence_stage human_biomarker_signal, momentum accelerating, and confidence low because the APRC-CM signal is single-arm, multimodal, component-confounded, and non-functional.

materiality_decision.rationale: The new Frontiers in Aging APRC-CM pilot changes the public context because it is a human healthy-aging biological-age source tied to a conditioned-media protocol. It does not strengthen the stage beyond human_biomarker_signal because the trial was small, single-arm, open-label, and bundled conditioned media with lifestyle and supplements. No coverage assessment update is needed because the latest coverage assessment already rates the track adequate and identifies product characterization, functional human benefit, and safety/durability as the main unresolved gaps.

search_log.summary: Scoped report found 4 registry-linked interventional studies, 0 missing detail records, 4 stale or missing registry checks, 0 open stale watch items, and 4 locally posted-result records.

NCT04388982 remains unknown status with no posted registry results; registry last update posted June 25, 2021; enrollment 9 estimated; phase 1/2; start July 1, 2020 actual; primary completion August 2021 estimated; completion August 2022 estimated.

The hUC-MSC exosome OA article reports trial number MR-13-24-017929 registered February 11, 2023 at medicalresearch.org.cn. The placental MSC-EV OA publication reports IRCT20210423051054N1 protocol approval on May 11, 2022. ANZCTR ACTRN12620000944932 is completed, last updated March 26, 2021, last data collection January 15, 2021, final sample size 11, with results provided March 26, 2021 and the 2023 publication automatically linked.

search_log.query: ((extracellular vesicle OR extracellular vesicles OR exosome OR exosomes OR secretome OR conditioned medium) AND (aging OR ageing OR rejuvenation OR senescence OR frailty OR healthspan OR Alzheimer OR osteoarthritis OR wound OR skin)) with publication-date or Entrez-date window 2026-06-06 to 2026-06-15
search_log.summary: Returned 57 PubMed hits. Close records included PMID 42272606 umbilical-cord mesenchymal secretome knee-OA study, PMID 42284014 anti-aging stem cell/EV review, PMID 42277017 cell-free wound-repair EV review, PMID 42271436 Wharton's jelly MSC skin rejuvenation study, PMID 42266813 facial scar exosome case report, PMID 42286377 correction to a mouse exercise-EV Alzheimer's paper, and multiple preclinical wound, osteoarthritis, senescence, or skin records. None besides the Frontiers APRC-CM article changed the public outlook context.

search_log.query: Supporting PMIDs 33728821, 25324650, 36396862, 37377400, 37859748, 37353884, 39465400, and 40500748 with retraction, erratum, expression-of-concern, or corrected-and-republished publication types
search_log.summary: Returned zero correction, retraction, erratum, expression-of-concern, or corrected-and-republished records for the eight supporting baseline PMIDs.

search_log.query: (exosome OR extracellular vesicle OR secretome OR conditioned medium) AND (aging OR older OR Alzheimer OR dementia OR osteoarthritis OR wound OR skin OR frailty OR rejuvenation)
search_log.summary: Broad registry search found multiple adjacent or no-results registries, including NCT06495437 age-related phenotype EV infusion with impaired glucose tolerance, NCT07322224 multimodal longevity protocol with APRC-CM, NCT07638813 new frontotemporal dementia MSC-exosome registry, NCT07221773 cosmetic scalp exosome registry, and sinusitis, autism, kidney disease, low-back-pain, OA, wound, and biomarker records. Only NCT07322224 had a same-window publication that changed public context.

Returned same-window exosome registry updates including NCT07221773 scalp microneedling, NCT07635433 chronic sinusitis nasal spray, NCT07638813 FTD MSC-exosome, NCT07304440 autism, NCT07587047 low-back pain, NCT07635459 sinusitis, and NCT07572890 kidney disease. None had posted results; NCT07638813 is relevant future watch context but not public evidence yet.

search_log.query: NCT07322224, APRC-CM, Multi-Modal Longevity Protocol, Frontiers Aging 10.3389/fragi.2026.1732426, IRCT20210423051054N1, ACTRN12620000944932, MR-13-24-017929
search_log.summary: Confirmed the Frontiers accepted article for the APRC-CM multimodal healthy-aging pilot; the International Journal of Hematology-Oncology and Stem Cell Research abstract for PMID 42272606; ANZCTR ACTRN12620000944932 trial dates, completion, and results-provided metadata; and publisher/source registration context for MR-13-24-017929 and IRCT20210423051054N1.

trial_watch_checks.summary: ClinicalTrials.gov remains unknown with no posted registry results, while the peer-reviewed Alzheimer's phase I/II source is already public. Staged action only refreshes registry metadata.

The peer-reviewed hUC-MSC exosome OA pilot remains the public result source; the article reports trial number MR-13-24-017929 and registration on February 11, 2023. Staged action only adds a registry check date.

The placental MSC-EV OA trial remains represented by its peer-reviewed placebo-controlled publication; the publication reports protocol approval on May 11, 2022 under IRCT20210423051054N1. Staged action only adds a registry check date.
ANZCTR confirms completed status, last data collection January 15, 2021, final sample size 11, and results-provided date March 26, 2021. Staged action refreshes dates and registry metadata.

ClinicalTrials.gov has no posted registry results, but a June 2026 Frontiers in Aging accepted article reports a single-arm APRC-CM multimodal healthy-aging biomarker signal. Staged action adds the source, study, finding, intervention, and outlook refresh.

New ClinicalTrials.gov registry for intranasal umbilical-cord MSC exosomes in frontotemporal dementia; not yet recruiting, 33 estimated participants, phase 1/2, no posted results. It is future watch context but not public evidence yet.

excluded_sources.title: Allogenic Umbilical Cord Mesenchymal Secretome for Knee Osteoarthritis: A Clinical Efficacy, Imaging and Safety Study.
excluded_sources.reason: Relevant disease-specific secretome clinical source, but it is a 10-patient uncontrolled knee-OA trial with within-subject functional and MRI T2 changes. The current public outlook already covers small uncontrolled OA signals and controlled OA null evidence; this source does not change stage or interpretation in this surveillance pass.

excluded_sources.title: Stem Cell and Stem Cell-Derived Extracellular Vesicles: Therapeutic Potential and Mechanistic Insights in Anti-Aging.
excluded_sources.reason: Review-level anti-aging context for stem cells and EVs; useful for future review triangulation but not a primary result that changes the evidence map.

excluded_sources.title: Stem cell-derived extracellular vesicles and artificial nanovesicles: a translational framework for cell-free wound repair.
excluded_sources.reason: Review-level wound-repair translation context, not a new human efficacy or safety result.

excluded_sources.title: Multi-omic analysis reveals Wharton's jelly mesenchymal stem cells shift the pro-inflammatory phenotype of fibroblast subpopulation to induce skin rejuvenation.
excluded_sources.reason: Skin rejuvenation context that appears centered on whole Wharton's jelly MSCs and fibroblast phenotypes rather than a product-defined EV or secretome therapy result that changes the current outlook.

excluded_sources.title: Scar Remodeling With Adipose-derived Stem Cell Exosomes After Facial Deep Dermal Injury.
excluded_sources.reason: Facial scar exosome case report; too uncontrolled and injury-specific to change the public EV/secretome aging interpretation.

excluded_sources.title: Author Correction: Exercise alleviates cognitive dysfunction in Alzheimer's disease mice via skeletal muscle-derived extracellular vesicles that enhance plaque clearance by microglia.
excluded_sources.reason: Correction to a mouse exercise-EV Alzheimer's source that is not part of the public baseline support map.

excluded_sources.title: Preliminary Safety and Efficacy Study of Extracellular Vesicle Infusion in the Intervention of Age-related Phenotypes With Impaired Glucose Tolerance.
excluded_sources.reason: Highly relevant aging-phenotype EV registry, but it remains not yet recruiting with no posted results and an old July 19, 2024 registry update; no same-window public evidence change.

excluded_sources.title: MSC-Exosome Therapy for Frontotemporal Dementia
excluded_sources.reason: Relevant new disease-specific exosome registry, but it is not yet recruiting, has no posted results, and should be watched rather than staged as evidence.

excluded_sources.title: Pilot Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Xvie Delivered Into the Scalp by Microneedling in Women With Self-perceived Thinning Hair
excluded_sources.reason: Cosmetic scalp/hair context with no posted results; adjacent to commercial exosome claims but not material to the current public aging interpretation.

excluded_sources.title: Thermosensitive Gel Nasal Spray With Stem Cell Exosomes, Mupirocin, and DNase I for Chronic Sinusitis
excluded_sources.reason: Sinusitis combination-product registry with no posted results; wrong disease context for this track's public aging interpretation.

notes: The pass began from the trial-watch stale registry cluster, but same-window source discovery found a public-context change that required staging rather than a direct live metadata edit.
The staged bundle intentionally keeps the EV/secretome public read tentative: the Frontiers APRC-CM pilot is directly aging-relevant but not component-isolating or controlled.
The scoped live trial-watch report will continue to show the four stale EV records until the staged bundle is reviewed and published.
next_actions: Run source_fidelity and interpretation_forecast reviews for extracellular-vesicle-and-secretome-therapies-surveillance-2026-06-15.
If approved and published, re-run npm run audit:trials -- --track extracellular-vesicle-and-secretome-therapies --write and then the global trial-watch audit.
Continue ordinary surveillance for NCT06495437 and NCT07638813 because both are relevant EV/secretome registries without posted results.
